item.title: Wall Street futures drop after US-Iran peace talks fail
item.description: Wall ​Street futures slipped on Monday, starting the week on a subdued note, after ‌talks between the U.S. and Iran over the weekend failed to deliver a deal to end the war.

item.title: Volkswagen posts weak start to year as China, US demand slumps further
item.description: Volkswagen ​reported a dip in global deliveries ‌at the start of the year, falling 4% year on year in the first three ​months of 2026, as the German ​carmaker battles with weak demand in ⁠China and the United States.

item.title: Audi Q1 deliveries fall 6.1% on weak demand in China and North America
item.description: Volkswagen's premium brand Audi on Monday said its first-quarter deliveries fell 6.1%, ​weighed down by weaker demand in ‌China and North America.

item.title: Exclusive: Chilean copper miner Codelco, contractors fined after deadly mine collapse
item.description: Chilean state copper miner Codelco was fined by labor authorities after last year's deadly collapse at its El Teniente mine, while three contractors whose workers were killed or injured were sanctioned more heavily, according to inspection records obtained by Reuters through public-records requests.

item.title: China poised for Q1 GDP growth rebound but Iran war dims 2026 outlook: Reuters poll
item.description: China's economy likely regained some momentum in the first quarter on solid ‌exports, but growth is expected to cool over the rest of 2026 as the Middle East crisis threatens to choke corporate profits and sap overseas demand, a Reuters poll showed.

item.title: World Bank chief sounds alarm about looming jobs crisis even after war ends
item.description: The Middle East war will dominate global finance officials' talks this week in Washington, but World Bank President Ajay Banga is sounding ​the alarm about a bigger, looming crisis: a huge gap in jobs for the 1.2 billion people who will reach working age in developing ‌countries in the next 10 to 15 years.

item.title: Chinese AI startup StepFun to unwind offshore structure to pave way for IPO, sources say
item.description: Chinese AI agent StepFun is unwinding its offshore incorporation structure to pave the way for a planned Hong Kong initial public offering, three sources said, ​as Beijing tightens scrutiny of a system widely used for overseas fundraising.

item.title: Basic-Fit data breach exposes details of gym members in Netherlands, elsewhere
item.description: Gym operator Basic-Fit has suffered a data breach involving "active members ​in several countries" and ​affecting around 200,000 of its clients ⁠in the Netherlands alone, ​it said on Monday.

item.title: UK fintech firm Wise's quarterly cross-border volumes surge ahead of Nasdaq debut
item.description: British fintech group Wise said ‌on Monday cross-border transaction volumes rose 26% in the fourth quarter to 49.4 billion ​pounds ($66.2 billion), reinforcing its expectation that ​annual profit margins will land near ⁠the top end of its forecast ​range.

item.title: Baker Hughes sells Waygate unit to Hexagon for about $1.45 billion
item.description: Oilfield services provider Baker Hughes sold its Waygate Technologies unit ​to Swedish industrial technology group Hexagon ‌for about $1.45 billion in cash, the two companies said on Monday.

item.title: Exclusive: Luxury brands face profits squeeze as Iran conflict shrinks Dubai Mall sales
item.description: Sales at Europe's biggest luxury brands have shrunk in Dubai and Abu Dhabi as the Iran conflict hit the sector's fastest-growing market in the latest setback for the $400 billion industry ​whose value has contracted over the last three years.

item.title: China's exports set to lose momentum as Iran war undercuts AI-driven boom: Reuters poll
item.description: China's export engine likely slowed in March ​as buyers chasing an AI-fuelled future confront the hard reality of war in the Middle East, which ‌has sparked an energy shock and revived market anxiety of past Gulf conflicts.

item.title: Morning Bid: Oil surges on US blockade of Iran
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Tom Westbrook

item.title: TSMC likely to book fourth straight quarter of record profit on insatiable AI demand
item.description: TSMC, the world's largest manufacturer of advanced artificial intelligence chips, will likely notch ‌up a fourth consecutive quarter of record earnings with a 50% surge in net profit for January-March thanks to booming demand for AI infrastructure.

item.title: Bank of Korea governor nominee vows to respond to excessive won weakness
item.description: The Bank of Korea's governor nominee said it would be necessary to ​respond properly if the won fell excessively, in written remarks he ‌submitted on Monday to parliament ahead of a parliamentary confirmation hearing on Wednesday.

item.title: McDonald's to add energy drinks, crafted sodas to US menus, WSJ reports
item.description: McDonald's is preparing to introduce a Red ​Bull Dragonberry Energizer as part of ‌a broader revamp of its cold drink menu at its U.S. restaurants later this year, the Wall Street ​Journal reported on Sunday, citing company ​documents.

item.title: GFL nears deal to buy Secure Waste for over $4.3 billion, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Waste management company GFL Environmental is nearing a deal to ​acquire Canada's Secure Waste Infrastructure ‌in a deal valued at over C$6 billion ($4.33 billion), Bloomberg News reported on ​Sunday, citing a person familiar ​with the matter.

item.title: Meatpacker JBS reaches tentative agreement with striking Colorado workers
item.description: Workers at JBS ratified a tentative two-year agreement with the world's largest meat company, ​covering nearly 3,800 workers at the JBS flagship beef ‌processing plant in Greeley, Colorado, according to statements from both the union and the company on Sunday.

item.title: Dollar strengthens as peace talks falter, US blockade of Iran's ports to begin
item.description: The dollar strengthened to the highest level in a week in ​a broad rally against most of its ‌peers in early Asian trade on Monday as peace talks between the U.S. and Iran broke down and the U.S. ​Navy prepared a blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: China's Victory Giant seeks $2.2 billion in Hong Kong listing amid market turmoil
item.description: China's Victory Giant launched a Hong Kong share sale on Monday seeking to raise up ‌to HK$17.49 billion ($2.23 billion), pressing ahead with what could rank among the city's largest listings this year despite global market turmoil driven by Middle East tensions.

item.title: Dollar jumps as failed US-Iran peace talks spark safe-haven push
item.description: US stock futures fell more than 1% in late Sunday U.S. trading.

item.title: Oil bounces back above $100 after US, Iran talks end in stalemate
item.description: Oil prices jumped above $100 ​a barrel on ‌Monday as the U.S. Navy prepared ​to blockade the ​Strait of Hormuz after ⁠talks between ​the U.S. and Iran ​failed to reach a deal to end ​the war.

item.title: US FTC in settlement talks with ad companies in boycott probe, WSJ reports
item.description: The U.S. Federal Trade ​Commission is ‌negotiating a potential settlement with ​several major ​advertising companies to resolve ⁠a probe ​into whether ​they violated federal antitrust laws by ​coordinating boycotts ​against platforms, including Elon ‌Musk’s ⁠X, the Wall Street Journal reported on ​Sunday, ​citing ⁠people familiar with the ​matter.

item.title: Dollar jumps as failed US/Iran peace talks spark fresh safe-haven push
item.description: The dollar jumped against other major currencies in thin trading late on Sunday, as investors sought the relative safety of the U.S. currency after marathon talks between ​Washington and Tehran failed to yield a peace deal, plunging markets into a seventh ‌week of uncertainty.

item.title: GSK sees blockbuster potential in targeted cancer therapy after promising early data
item.description: British drugmaker GSK's experimental targeted cancer drug Mo-rez has blockbuster potential, its head of oncology research has told reporters, after early data showed the drug helped ​shrink tumors in patients with advanced, hard-to-treat cancers.

item.title: UK financial regulators rush to assess risks of Anthropic’s latest AI model, FT reports
item.description: UK financial regulators ​are holding ‌urgent talks with ​the ​government's cyber security agency ⁠and ​major banks ​to assess risks posed by ​the ​new artificial intelligence ‌model ⁠from Anthropic, the Financial Times ​reported ​on ⁠Sunday.

item.title: China's Chery looking to expand car production in Europe, top executives say
item.description: Chery is looking to expand car production in Europe through partnerships with other automakers allowing it to use existing factories, top executives at the Chinese carmaker said ​at an event in Paris.

item.title: Economic shock of Middle East war to cast shadow over IMF, World Bank meetings
item.description: Top finance officials from around the world will convene in Washington this week under the shadow of the war in the Middle East, which has delivered a third major shock to the global economy after the COVID ​pandemic and Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

item.title: Iran aims to restore majority of refining capability within two months, oil ministry official says
item.description: Iran expects to restore most ​damaged refining and ‌distribution facilities to 70–80% of their pre-attack ​capacity within one ​to two months, a ⁠senior oil official ​said, as authorities ​work to recover from a wave of strikes on ​energy infrastructure.

item.title: Saudi Arabia restores full capacity on East-West oil pipeline to 7 million bpd after attacks
item.description: Saudi Arabia has restored full oil pumping capacity through the East-West ​pipeline to about seven million barrels per day, it ‌said on Sunday, days after providing an assessment of damage on its energy sector from attacks during the Iran conflict.
